title: A Wooden Box Earned $1,000 With Zero Musicians #Shorts
desc:
In 1889, Louis Glass bolted a coin slot onto Edison's new phonograph and put it in a San Francisco saloon — no bartender, no musician, just a nickel and a listening tube. In six months it earned over $1,000, and that one box became the first jukebox, growing into a billion-dollar coin-operated music industry.

#BrokeToBuilt #history #invention #jukebox #shorts
